title: campium bipinnatifidum
intro: Campium bipinnatifidum, also known as the "cut-leaved campion," is a flowering plant species in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of the northern hemisphere. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Campium bipinnatifidum are numerous, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and can also be used topically in the form of a salve or poultice.
